---
name: caravan-drive-for-devs
description: "Caravan (drive for devs) v0.1.0 is built and validated — Go CLI at fable-experiments/drive-for-devs/caravan, deployed on the Mac mini"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: f507fe17-d781-4a81-8f64-e112b7e8148f
---

Caravan v0.1.0 (built 2026-07-02) lives at `~/fable-experiments/drive-for-devs/caravan` (its own git repo; module `caravan`, Go 1.23/1.24). Commands: `init`, `up`, `status`, `secrets` (native age via filippo.io/age — deliberate deviation from PLAN.md's SOPS since neither sops nor age is installed on either machine), and `sync` — a snapshot-based three-way bidirectional folder sync over ssh+tar with newer-mtime-wins conflicts, delete propagation, and self-bootstrap (scp's its own binary to remote `~/.local/bin/caravan`).

State on the Mac mini (`ssh m@ms-mac-mini.tail901772.ts.net`, macOS arm64 like the MacBook): binary at `~/.local/bin/caravan`, test manifest at `~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml`, provisioned test workspace at `~/caravan-test-workspace`, sync test folder `~/caravan-test-sync` paired with the same path locally.

Validation: 71 unit/integration tests (`go test ./...`), cross-device e2e `test/e2e-sync.sh` 13/13 (push/pull/delete/conflict both directions, sha256 tree parity). Cold `caravan up` on the mini: 1.1s for two repos.

v0.1.1 (same day): remote binaries now self-update via a version handshake on every sync scan; flags can appear anywhere in the arg list (internal/cliargs); `sandbox/` has a tested Dockerfile+devcontainer recipe (container cold-start provision ~1.3s); cross-device secrets add-machine flow validated both directions; stress numbers: 1003 files/15MB push 8.9s, no-op scan 0.4s, incremental 1.0s. Sync base state lives in `~/.config/caravan/sync-state/`.

v0.1.2: three real bugs found by probes and fixed — (1) fresh clone skipped secrets/direnv/mise entirely (early return in processRepo; the flagship fresh-machine flow was broken), (2) file↔dir type flips wedged the sync (planner now emits pre-delete ops + child suppression), (3) unicode filenames ping-ponged forever because macOS tar extraction stores NFD while local scan reported NFC — scan keys now NFC-normalized. mise integration validated in Docker (sandbox/Dockerfile.mise, jq via file:// fixture repo). test/edge-sync.sh is a 10-round cross-device edge probe (28/28): spaces, unicode, deep nesting, empty dirs, type flips, quote-skip, delete-vs-modify, renames.

v0.2.0: opt-in `checksum = true` per sync entry (sha256 change detection, closes the size+mtime blind spot — proven cross-device); `caravan daemon install/uninstall/status` runs sync as a launchd LaunchAgent (validated live, ≤12s propagation both directions, logs in ~/Library/Logs/caravan/); per-entry flock prevents daemon/manual races; remotes lacking new flags self-update. A dogfood-ready manifest now lives at ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml locally (test-folder entry); daemon left UNINSTALLED — enable with `caravan daemon install test-folder --interval 5s`.

v0.3.0: conflict losers backed up to ~/.config/caravan/conflicts/<entry>/ on the losing machine (7-day pruning) — newer-wins never destroys data; `delta_min_bytes` (default 8MiB) routes large files through rsync delta (40MB+1KB append: 1.8s vs 12s full; openrsync interop verified); release tooling (Makefile, scripts/release.sh 4-platform, scripts/install.sh, Homebrew template). The /loop session is a standing improvement loop — user re-invokes it to continue building.

v0.4.0: ssh ControlMaster multiplexing everywhere (warm no-op sync 0.05s); `scan --wait` remote long-poll + reworked watch loop (per-entry goroutines, 1s local poll + 20s remote long-poll) → 0.6–1.2s propagation either direction. Live testing caught a 30s blind window (long-poll error backoff blocked local polling on stale remotes) — fixed: errors trigger an immediate healing pass and never block local polls; validated by downgrading the mini to 0.2.0 (heal+propagate 0.7s).

v0.5.0: `caravan doctor` diagnostics; chmod propagation (found live: in-sync passes now refresh the base when scans carry metadata the stored base lacks — otherwise mode/hash-gated features stayed dormant on existing pairs); hub-and-spoke topology validated 10/10 (MacBook↔mini-hub↔replica-B on mini via its own launchd daemon; 0.5–1.0s A→hub→B propagation incl. deletes and cross-replica conflicts; test/topology-sync.sh).

v0.5.1: chaos probe (test/chaos-sync.sh) found a DATA-LOSS bug — SIGKILL mid-transfer left a tar-written partial in the tree and the post-apply rescan recorded it into the base → permanent silent divergence. Fixed: tar receives stage into <root>/.caravan-staging + rename into place; copyFile writes tmp+rename; buildBase skips size-mismatched pairs. All 6 chaos scenarios green post-fix (kill mid-push/pull 200MB, mux drop, corrupt/missing state, 5-way locks, 10k files: 11s push/<1s no-op).

Next per PLAN.md: Phase 1 dogfood (2-4 weeks as exclusive provisioning) — the tool is feature-complete AND chaos-hardened for Tier 0+1. Remaining accepted limitations: single-quote/newline filenames skipped, NFD-on-Linux unsupported. Future (needs user's call or dogfood signal): GitHub repo + hosted releases (outward-facing), JuiceFS Tier 2 evaluation, FSEvents kernel events if 1s poll ever matters.

Pushed — all 10 commits (v0.1.0 through v0.5.1 plus docs) are live on `main` at **github.com/itsmaleen/caravan**, with the local branch tracking `origin/main`. I also updated the README's clone instructions to point at the real URL before pushing.

Two things worth knowing since the repo is **public**:

1. **Your tailnet hostname is in the test scripts** (`m@ms-mac-mini.tail901772.ts.net` in `test/*.sh` and docs). Tailscale hostnames aren't reachable from outside your tailnet, so it's not a security hole, but if you'd rather not advertise it I can parameterize the scripts to read the host purely from `$CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE` with no default.
2. **Hosted releases are now unblocked.** The `packaging/homebrew/caravan.rb` formula and `scripts/install.sh --url` were built with placeholders waiting for a public home. When you want, I can cut a `v0.5.1` GitHub release with the 4-platform tarballs from `make release` and fill in the formula's real URL + sha256.

The hourly soak loop continues in the background — next health check fires at 12:30.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/marlin/.claude/skills/efficient-fable

# Efficient Fable

Use Claude Fable as the orchestrator, architect, synthesizer, and final judge.
Use cheaper subagents for token-heavy research, coding, testing, and
summarization that do not require Fable's full judgment.

## Where Fable Shines

Reserve Fable for:

- Decomposing ambiguous work into clean parallel slices.
- Architecture, product, and safety tradeoffs.
- Reading conflicting subagent reports and deciding what matters.
- Integrating partial implementations into one coherent plan.
- Final review, risk assessment, and user-facing synthesis.

## Delegation Pattern

1. Name the expensive-token risk: large repo search, long logs, broad docs, or
   repetitive edits.
2. Split independent work into subagents before reading everything yourself.
3. Use cheaper models for research scans, inventory, search summaries, narrow
   bug hunts, browser/testing passes, test output reduction, and bounded code
   edits.
4. Ask subagents for concise evidence: files, line references, commands run,
   diffs, uncertainties, and stop conditions they hit.
5. Spend Fable tokens on the decision layer: compare results, resolve conflicts,
   choose the implementation path, and review the final patch.

Prefer parallel subagents when the slices do not depend on each other. Keep
blocking or highly coupled work local.

## Handoff Packets

Write delegated prompts as if the subagent has no useful chat context. Include
only the context it needs:

- The repo path and exact objective.
- The files, packages, or surfaces in scope and anything explicitly out of
  scope.
- The evidence format to return: files, line refs, commands, diffs, failures,
  screenshots, and uncertainty.
- The verification commands or browser flows to run, plus what success should
  look like when that is knowable.
- Stop conditions: if the code does not match the prompt, a command fails after
  a reasonable retry, or the task needs out-of-scope files, stop and report
  instead of improvising.

## Vetting Delegated Work

Treat subagent reports as leads, not facts. Before using a high-impact finding,
opening a PR, or telling the user the work is done, Fable should reopen the
important cited files, confirm the relevant line refs or failures, and review
the final diff against the task. Let lighter agents gather signal; keep
truth-judgment with Fable.

## Common Scenarios

Treat these as soft defaults, not rigid rules:

- Research: ask lighter agents to scan docs, prior art, APIs, and repo surfaces;
  Fable decides what evidence changes the plan.
- Coding: give cheaper agents bounded edits or candidate patches; Fable owns
  shared-file coordination, integration, and final review.
- Testing: have Fable suggest the validation direction and the scripts or
  browser checks that matter. Let lighter agents run targeted tests, browser
  flows, screenshots, and log reduction, then report exact commands, failures,
  likely causes, and whether failures look flaky, environmental, or real.
- Debugging: use cheaper agents to cluster logs, reproduce issues, and try
  small fixes; Fable decides which diagnosis is most trustworthy.

If a task is tiny or the validation itself needs delicate judgment, keep it
with Fable.

## Diagram

Use `assets/fable-orchestrator.excalidraw` when a visual explanation helps.

## Claims

For codebase-heavy work, it is reasonable to describe this as up to 3-5x more
cost-efficient and 2-4x faster when independent research, coding, or testing
slices can run in parallel. Treat those as workload-dependent estimates, not
guarantees.

Good launch copy:

> Make Claude Fable more efficient by using cheaper subagents for token-heavy
> research, coding, and testing, saving Fable for judgment, architecture,
> synthesis, and final review.

for f in test/e2e-sync.sh test/stress-sync.sh test/edge-sync.sh test/chaos-sync.sh test/topology-sync.sh; do
python3 - "$f" <<'EOF'
import sys
p = sys.argv[1]
s = open(p).read()
old = 'MINI="${CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE:-m@ms-mac-mini.tail901772.ts.net}"'
new = '''MINI="${CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE:-}"
[ -n "$MINI" ] || { echo "usage: set CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=user@host (ssh target for the test remote)"; exit 2; }'''
assert old in s, p
s = s.replace(old, new)
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print("parameterized", p)
EOF
done

2	# Caravan sync stress test: 1000 small files + a few binary blobs, cross-device.
3	# Measures initial push, no-op scan, and incremental single-file propagation.
4	set -u
5	MINI="${CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE:-}"
6	[ -n "$MINI" ] || { echo "usage: set CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=user@host (ssh target for the test remote)"; exit 2; }
7	BIN="${CARAVAN_BIN:-$(pwd)/caravan}"
8	LOCAL_DIR="$HOME/caravan-stress-sync"
9	MANIFEST="$(mktemp -d)/caravan.toml"
10	
11	cat > "$MANIFEST" <<EOF
12	version = 1
13	[workspace]
14	root = "~/code"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Caravan cross-device e2e sync test.
3	# Runs from the caravan repo root on the primary machine; exercises bidirectional
4	# sync of a test folder against the Mac mini over ssh, asserting content parity
5	# after each mutation round.
6	set -u
7	
8	MINI="${CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE:-}"
9	[ -n "$MINI" ] || { echo "usage: set CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=user@host (ssh target for the test remote)"; exit 2; }
10	LOCAL_DIR="$HOME/caravan-test-sync"
11	REMOTE_DIR='caravan-test-sync'   # relative to remote $HOME
12	BIN="${CARAVAN_BIN:-$(pwd)/caravan}"
13	MANIFEST="$(mktemp -d)/caravan.toml"
14	STATE_DIR="$HOME/.config/caravan/sync-state"
15	
16	PASS=0; FAIL=0
17	ok()   { PASS=$((PASS+1)); echo "  ✓ $1"; }
18	bad()  { FAIL=$((FAIL+1)); echo "  ✗ $1"; }
19	check() { # check <desc> <cmd...>
20	  local desc="$1"; shift
21	  if "$@" >/dev/null 2>&1; then ok "$desc"; else bad "$desc"; fi
22	}
23	
24	# Tree fingerprint: relative path + sha256 of every file, sorted.
25	local_tree()  { (cd "$LOCAL_DIR" && find . -type f ! -name .DS_Store ! -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 shasum -a 256 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2, $1}'); }
26	remote_tree() { ssh -o BatchMode=yes "$MINI" "cd $REMOTE_DIR && find . -type f ! -name .DS_Store ! -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 shasum -a 256 2>/dev/null | awk '{print \$2, \$1}'"; }
27	
28	trees_match() {
29	  local l r
30	  l="$(local_tree)"; r="$(remote_tree)"
31	  if [ "$l" = "$r" ]; then return 0; else
32	    echo "--- local ---"; echo "$l"; echo "--- remote ---"; echo "$r"; return 1
33	  fi
34	}
35	
36	sync_once() { "$BIN" sync -f "$MANIFEST" test-folder; }
37	
38	echo "== setup =="
39	rm -rf "$LOCAL_DIR" "$STATE_DIR/test-folder.json"
40	ssh -o BatchMode=yes "$MINI" "rm -rf $REMOTE_DIR" || { echo "ssh unreachable"; exit 1; }
41	mkdir -p "$LOCAL_DIR/sub/deep" "$LOCAL_DIR/node_modules/fake"
42	cat > "$MANIFEST" <<EOF
43	version = 1
44	[workspace]
45	root = "~/code"
46	[[sync]]
47	name   = "test-folder"
48	local  = "~/caravan-test-sync"
49	remote = "$MINI:~/caravan-test-sync"
50	EOF
51	echo "hello from $(hostname)" > "$LOCAL_DIR/hello.txt"
52	echo "nested content"          > "$LOCAL_DIR/sub/deep/nested.txt"
53	echo "# notes"                 > "$LOCAL_DIR/notes.md"
54	echo "excluded"                > "$LOCAL_DIR/node_modules/fake/dep.js"
55	touch "$LOCAL_DIR/.DS_Store"
56	dd if=/dev/urandom of="$LOCAL_DIR/blob.bin" bs=1024 count=512 2>/dev/null
57	
58	echo "== round 1: initial push =="
59	sync_once || bad "sync exit code (initial push)"
60	check "trees match after initial push" trees_match
61	if ssh "$MINI" "test -e $REMOTE_DIR/node_modules"; then bad "excludes: node_modules leaked to remote"; else ok "excludes: node_modules not pushed"; fi
62	
63	echo "== round 2: no-op sync is clean =="
64	OUT="$(sync_once 2>&1)"; echo "$OUT" | grep -q "in sync" && ok "no-op reports in sync" || bad "no-op reports in sync (got: $OUT)"
65	
66	echo "== round 3: remote edits propagate back =="
67	sleep 2
68	ssh "$MINI" "echo 'edited on mini' >> $REMOTE_DIR/hello.txt && echo 'born on mini' > $REMOTE_DIR/remote-new.txt && rm $REMOTE_DIR/notes.md && mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR/mini-dir && echo x > $REMOTE_DIR/mini-dir/f.txt"
69	sync_once || bad "sync exit code (pull round)"
70	check "trees match after remote edits" trees_match
71	grep -q "edited on mini" "$LOCAL_DIR/hello.txt" && ok "remote modification pulled" || bad "remote modification pulled"
72	[ -f "$LOCAL_DIR/remote-new.txt" ] && ok "remote new file pulled" || bad "remote new file pulled"
73	[ ! -e "$LOCAL_DIR/notes.md" ] && ok "remote deletion propagated to local" || bad "remote deletion propagated to local"
74	
75	echo "== round 4: local edits propagate =="
76	sleep 2
77	echo "local addition" > "$LOCAL_DIR/local-new.txt"
78	rm "$LOCAL_DIR/remote-new.txt"
79	echo "local edit" >> "$LOCAL_DIR/sub/deep/nested.txt"
80	sync_once || bad "sync exit code (push round)"
81	check "trees match after local edits" trees_match
82	ssh "$MINI" "test ! -e $REMOTE_DIR/remote-new.txt" && ok "local deletion propagated to remote" || bad "local deletion propagated to remote"
83	
84	echo "== round 5: conflict — newer (remote) wins =="
85	sleep 2
86	echo "local version" > "$LOCAL_DIR/conflict.txt"
87	sleep 2
88	ssh "$MINI" "echo 'remote version' > $REMOTE_DIR/conflict.txt"
89	sync_once || bad "sync exit code (conflict round)"
90	check "trees match after conflict" trees_match
91	grep -q "remote version" "$LOCAL_DIR/conflict.txt" && ok "newer remote wins conflict" || bad "newer remote wins conflict (got: $(cat "$LOCAL_DIR/conflict.txt"))"
92	
93	echo "== round 6: conflict — newer (local) wins =="
94	sleep 2
95	ssh "$MINI" "echo 'remote v2' > $REMOTE_DIR/conflict.txt"
96	sleep 2
97	echo "local v2" > "$LOCAL_DIR/conflict.txt"
98	sync_once || bad "sync exit code (conflict round 2)"
99	check "trees match after conflict 2" trees_match
100	R="$(ssh "$MINI" "cat $REMOTE_DIR/conflict.txt")"
101	[ "$R" = "local v2" ] && ok "newer local wins conflict" || bad "newer local wins conflict (remote has: $R)"
102	
103	echo
104	echo "RESULT: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
105	[ "$FAIL" -eq 0 ]

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Caravan hub-and-spoke topology test: three replicas across two machines.
3	#
4	#   A  = MacBook  ~/caravan-topo-A          (this machine)
5	#   hub= mini     ~/caravan-topo-hub        (always-on hub)
6	#   B  = mini     ~/caravan-topo-B          (second replica, synced hub<->B by a
7	#                                            caravan watch RUNNING ON THE MINI
8	#                                            over the local: transport)
9	#
10	# A<->hub runs as a watch on this machine. Changes must flow A->hub->B and
11	# B->hub->A. Conflicts between A and B are mediated by the hub (newer wins,
12	# losers backed up on the losing side).
13	set -u
14	
15	MINI="${CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE:-}"
16	[ -n "$MINI" ] || { echo "usage: set CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=user@host (ssh target for the test remote)"; exit 2; }
17	BIN="${CARAVAN_BIN:-$(pwd)/caravan}"
18	A="$HOME/caravan-topo-A"
19	MAN_A="$(mktemp -d)/caravan.toml"
20	
21	PASS=0; FAIL=0
22	ok()  { PASS=$((PASS+1)); echo "  PASS: $1"; }
23	bad() { FAIL=$((FAIL+1)); echo "  FAIL: $1"; }
24	
25	# wait_for <desc> <timeout-s> <cmd...>  — poll until cmd succeeds
26	wait_for() {
27	  local desc="$1" timeout="$2"; shift 2
28	  local waited=0
29	  while ! "$@" >/dev/null 2>&1; do
30	    sleep 0.5; waited=$((waited+1))
31	    if [ "$waited" -ge $((timeout*2)) ]; then bad "$desc (timeout ${timeout}s)"; return 1; fi
32	  done
33	  ok "$desc ($(python3 -c "print(f'{$waited*0.5:.1f}')")s)"
34	}
35	
36	on_mini() { ssh -o BatchMode=yes "$MINI" "$@"; }
37	
38	echo "== setup =="
39	rm -rf "$A" "$HOME/.config/caravan/sync-state/topo-A.json"
40	on_mini 'rm -rf caravan-topo-hub caravan-topo-B ~/.config/caravan/sync-state/topo-B.json ~/Library/Logs/caravan/topo-B.log'
41	mkdir -p "$A"
42	
43	cat > "$MAN_A" <<EOF
44	version = 1
45	[workspace]
46	root = "~/code"
47	[[sync]]
48	name   = "topo-A"
49	local  = "~/caravan-topo-A"
50	remote = "$MINI:~/caravan-topo-hub"
51	EOF
52	
53	# Mini-side manifest: append the hub<->B entry if not present.
54	on_mini 'grep -q caravan-topo-B ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml 2>/dev/null || cat >> ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml <<EOF
55	
56	[[sync]]
57	name   = "topo-B"
58	local  = "~/caravan-topo-hub"
59	remote = "local:/Users/m/caravan-topo-B"
60	EOF'
61	
62	echo "== start hub<->B daemon on the mini =="
63	on_mini '~/.local/bin/caravan daemon install topo-B --interval 1s' && ok "daemon installed on mini" || bad "daemon install on mini"
64	sleep 2
65	on_mini '~/.local/bin/caravan daemon status topo-B' | grep -q "✓" && ok "mini daemon running" || bad "mini daemon running"
66	
67	echo "== start A<->hub watch locally =="
68	"$BIN" sync --watch -f "$MAN_A" topo-A > /tmp/topo-watch.log 2>&1 &
69	WPID=$!
70	sleep 3
71	
72	echo "== A -> hub -> B propagation =="
73	echo "born on A" > "$A/from-a.txt"
74	wait_for "from-a.txt reaches B via hub" 30 on_mini 'test -f caravan-topo-B/from-a.txt'
75	
76	echo "== B -> hub -> A propagation =="
77	on_mini 'echo "born on B" > caravan-topo-B/from-b.txt'
78	wait_for "from-b.txt reaches A via hub" 30 test -f "$A/from-b.txt"
79	
80	echo "== delete propagation A -> B =="
81	rm "$A/from-a.txt"
82	wait_for "deletion of from-a.txt reaches B" 30 on_mini 'test ! -e caravan-topo-B/from-a.txt'
83	
84	echo "== three-way content parity =="
85	sleep 3
86	echo "shared" > "$A/parity.txt"
87	wait_for "parity.txt reaches B" 30 on_mini 'test -f caravan-topo-B/parity.txt'
88	HA=$(shasum -a 256 "$A/parity.txt" | awk '{print $1}')
89	HH=$(on_mini 'shasum -a 256 caravan-topo-hub/parity.txt' | awk '{print $1}')
90	HB=$(on_mini 'shasum -a 256 caravan-topo-B/parity.txt' | awk '{print $1}')
91	[ "$HA" = "$HH" ] && [ "$HH" = "$HB" ] && ok "A, hub, B byte-identical" || bad "replica divergence: A=$HA hub=$HH B=$HB"
92	
93	echo "== A-vs-B conflict mediated by hub (B newer wins) =="
94	echo "A version" > "$A/clash.txt"
95	wait_for "clash.txt seeded to B" 30 on_mini 'test -f caravan-topo-B/clash.txt'
96	sleep 2
97	on_mini 'echo "B version wins" > caravan-topo-B/clash.txt'
98	wait_for "B version reaches A" 40 grep -q "B version wins" "$A/clash.txt"
99	
100	echo "== cleanup =="
101	kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
102	on_mini '~/.local/bin/caravan daemon uninstall topo-B' >/dev/null 2>&1 && ok "mini daemon uninstalled" || bad "mini daemon uninstall"
103	rm -rf "$A" "$HOME/.config/caravan/sync-state/topo-A.json"
104	on_mini 'rm -rf caravan-topo-hub caravan-topo-B ~/.config/caravan/sync-state/topo-B.json'
105	# strip the topo-B entry from the mini manifest again
106	on_mini 'python3 - <<PYEOF
107	import re
108	p = "/Users/m/.config/caravan/caravan.toml"
109	s = open(p).read()
110	s = re.sub(r"\n*\[\[sync\]\]\nname   = \"topo-B\"\nlocal  = \"~/caravan-topo-hub\"\nremote = \"local:/Users/m/caravan-topo-B\"\n?", "\n", s)
111	open(p, "w").write(s)
112	PYEOF'
113	
114	echo
115	echo "RESULT: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
116	[ "$FAIL" -eq 0 ]

input.command:
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
p = 'test/topology-sync.sh'
s = open(p).read()

old_append = '''# Mini-side manifest: append the hub<->B entry if not present.
on_mini 'grep -q caravan-topo-B ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml 2>/dev/null || cat >> ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml <<EOF

[[sync]]
name   = "topo-B"
local  = "~/caravan-topo-hub"
remote = "local:/Users/m/caravan-topo-B"
EOF'
'''
new_append = '''# Mini-side manifest: append the hub<->B entry if not present.
# (local: transport needs an absolute path; derive the remote home at runtime)
RHOME="$(on_mini 'echo $HOME')"
on_mini "grep -q caravan-topo-B ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml 2>/dev/null || cat >> ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml <<EOF

[[sync]]
name   = \\"topo-B\\"
local  = \\"~/caravan-topo-hub\\"
remote = \\"local:$RHOME/caravan-topo-B\\"
EOF"
'''
assert old_append in s
s = s.replace(old_append, new_append)

old_clean = '''on_mini 'python3 - <<PYEOF
import re
p = "/Users/m/.config/caravan/caravan.toml"
s = open(p).read()
s = re.sub(r"\\n*\\[\\[sync\\]\\]\\nname   = \\"topo-B\\"\\nlocal  = \\"~/caravan-topo-hub\\"\\nremote = \\"local:/Users/m/caravan-topo-B\\"\\n?", "\\n", s)
open(p, "w").write(s)
PYEOF'
'''
new_clean = '''on_mini 'python3 - <<PYEOF
import os, re
p = os.path.expanduser("~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml")
s = open(p).read()
s = re.sub(r"\\n*\\[\\[sync\\]\\]\\nname   = \\"topo-B\\"\\nlocal  = \\"~/caravan-topo-hub\\"\\nremote = \\"local:[^\\"]*caravan-topo-B\\"\\n?", "\\n", s)
open(p, "w").write(s)
PYEOF'
'''
assert old_clean in s
s = s.replace(old_clean, new_clean)
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print("topology parameterized")
PYEOF

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: caravan-drive-for-devs
description: "Caravan (drive for devs) v0.1.0 is built and validated — Go CLI at fable-experiments/drive-for-devs/caravan, deployed on the Mac mini"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: f507fe17-d781-4a81-8f64-e112b7e8148f
---

Caravan v0.1.0 (built 2026-07-02) lives at `~/fable-experiments/drive-for-devs/caravan` (its own git repo; module `caravan`, Go 1.23/1.24). Commands: `init`, `up`, `status`, `secrets` (native age via filippo.io/age — deliberate deviation from PLAN.md's SOPS since neither sops nor age is installed on either machine), and `sync` — a snapshot-based three-way bidirectional folder sync over ssh+tar with newer-mtime-wins conflicts, delete propagation, and self-bootstrap (scp's its own binary to remote `~/.local/bin/caravan`).

State on the Mac mini (`ssh m@ms-mac-mini.tail901772.ts.net`, macOS arm64 like the MacBook): binary at `~/.local/bin/caravan`, test manifest at `~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml`, provisioned test workspace at `~/caravan-test-workspace`, sync test folder `~/caravan-test-sync` paired with the same path locally.

Validation: 71 unit/integration tests (`go test ./...`), cross-device e2e `test/e2e-sync.sh` 13/13 (push/pull/delete/conflict both directions, sha256 tree parity). Cold `caravan up` on the mini: 1.1s for two repos.

v0.1.1 (same day): remote binaries now self-update via a version handshake on every sync scan; flags can appear anywhere in the arg list (internal/cliargs); `sandbox/` has a tested Dockerfile+devcontainer recipe (container cold-start provision ~1.3s); cross-device secrets add-machine flow validated both directions; stress numbers: 1003 files/15MB push 8.9s, no-op scan 0.4s, incremental 1.0s. Sync base state lives in `~/.config/caravan/sync-state/`.

v0.1.2: three real bugs found by probes and fixed — (1) fresh clone skipped secrets/direnv/mise entirely (early return in processRepo; the flagship fresh-machine flow was broken), (2) file↔dir type flips wedged the sync (planner now emits pre-delete ops + child suppression), (3) unicode filenames ping-ponged forever because macOS tar extraction stores NFD while local scan reported NFC — scan keys now NFC-normalized. mise integration validated in Docker (sandbox/Dockerfile.mise, jq via file:// fixture repo). test/edge-sync.sh is a 10-round cross-device edge probe (28/28): spaces, unicode, deep nesting, empty dirs, type flips, quote-skip, delete-vs-modify, renames.

v0.2.0: opt-in `checksum = true` per sync entry (sha256 change detection, closes the size+mtime blind spot — proven cross-device); `caravan daemon install/uninstall/status` runs sync as a launchd LaunchAgent (validated live, ≤12s propagation both directions, logs in ~/Library/Logs/caravan/); per-entry flock prevents daemon/manual races; remotes lacking new flags self-update. A dogfood-ready manifest now lives at ~/.config/caravan/caravan.toml locally (test-folder entry); daemon left UNINSTALLED — enable with `caravan daemon install test-folder --interval 5s`.

v0.3.0: conflict losers backed up to ~/.config/caravan/conflicts/<entry>/ on the losing machine (7-day pruning) — newer-wins never destroys data; `delta_min_bytes` (default 8MiB) routes large files through rsync delta (40MB+1KB append: 1.8s vs 12s full; openrsync interop verified); release tooling (Makefile, scripts/release.sh 4-platform, scripts/install.sh, Homebrew template). The /loop session is a standing improvement loop — user re-invokes it to continue building.

v0.4.0: ssh ControlMaster multiplexing everywhere (warm no-op sync 0.05s); `scan --wait` remote long-poll + reworked watch loop (per-entry goroutines, 1s local poll + 20s remote long-poll) → 0.6–1.2s propagation either direction. Live testing caught a 30s blind window (long-poll error backoff blocked local polling on stale remotes) — fixed: errors trigger an immediate healing pass and never block local polls; validated by downgrading the mini to 0.2.0 (heal+propagate 0.7s).

v0.5.0: `caravan doctor` diagnostics; chmod propagation (found live: in-sync passes now refresh the base when scans carry metadata the stored base lacks — otherwise mode/hash-gated features stayed dormant on existing pairs); hub-and-spoke topology validated 10/10 (MacBook↔mini-hub↔replica-B on mini via its own launchd daemon; 0.5–1.0s A→hub→B propagation incl. deletes and cross-replica conflicts; test/topology-sync.sh).

v0.5.1: chaos probe (test/chaos-sync.sh) found a DATA-LOSS bug — SIGKILL mid-transfer left a tar-written partial in the tree and the post-apply rescan recorded it into the base → permanent silent divergence. Fixed: tar receives stage into <root>/.caravan-staging + rename into place; copyFile writes tmp+rename; buildBase skips size-mismatched pairs. All 6 chaos scenarios green post-fix (kill mid-push/pull 200MB, mux drop, corrupt/missing state, 5-way locks, 10k files: 11s push/<1s no-op).

Next per PLAN.md: Phase 1 dogfood (2-4 weeks as exclusive provisioning) — the tool is feature-complete AND chaos-hardened for Tier 0+1. Remaining accepted limitations: single-quote/newline filenames skipped, NFD-on-Linux unsupported. Published 2026-07-02 to git@github.com:itsmaleen/caravan.git (PUBLIC, main). Test scripts embed the tailnet hostname (not publicly routable, user aware). Now unblocked: hosted release tarballs + filling in packaging/homebrew/caravan.rb placeholders. Future: JuiceFS Tier 2 evaluation, FSEvents kernel events if 1s poll ever matters.

aiTitle: Set up git repo and push to GitHub

content.text:
Done — the hostname is gone from the code and from every commit in history, verified against a fresh clone from GitHub.

What changed:

1. **Parameterized**: all five test scripts now require `CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=user@host` and fail fast with a usage message if it's unset — no embedded default. The topology test also derives the remote `$HOME` at runtime instead of hardcoding a path. Verified working: the full e2e still passes 13/13 with the var set.
2. **History rewritten**: `git filter-branch` scrubbed `tail901772.ts.net` (and the hardcoded remote-home paths) from every historical blob, backup refs and reflog purged, then force-pushed. All commit IDs changed as a result. A fresh clone from GitHub greps clean across the entire history.

Two follow-on notes:

- **Running the cross-device tests now needs the env var**: `CARAVAN_TEST_REMOTE=m@ms-mac-mini.tail901772.ts.net ./test/e2e-sync.sh` — I've noted this in memory so future soak passes use it.
- **One residual caveat**: GitHub can keep the old (pre-rewrite) commits reachable by direct SHA on their servers until their internal GC runs, since the repo was public for a short while. Given it's a non-routable tailnet hostname that was up for minutes, that's almost certainly fine — but if you want it fully purged server-side, the formal path is a GitHub support request to run GC on the repo.

The hourly soak loop continues; next check ~12:30.

content: Building out caravan, a workspace sync CLI, now at v0.5.1 and published to GitHub with the tailnet hostname scrubbed from all history. Next action: hourly soak health check of MacBook-to-mini sync fires around 12:30.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/marlin/.claude/skills/efficient-fable

# Efficient Fable

Use Claude Fable as the orchestrator, architect, synthesizer, and final judge.
Use cheaper subagents for token-heavy research, coding, testing, and
summarization that do not require Fable's full judgment.

## Where Fable Shines

Reserve Fable for:

- Decomposing ambiguous work into clean parallel slices.
- Architecture, product, and safety tradeoffs.
- Reading conflicting subagent reports and deciding what matters.
- Integrating partial implementations into one coherent plan.
- Final review, risk assessment, and user-facing synthesis.

## Delegation Pattern

1. Name the expensive-token risk: large repo search, long logs, broad docs, or
   repetitive edits.
2. Split independent work into subagents before reading everything yourself.
3. Use cheaper models for research scans, inventory, search summaries, narrow
   bug hunts, browser/testing passes, test output reduction, and bounded code
   edits.
4. Ask subagents for concise evidence: files, line references, commands run,
   diffs, uncertainties, and stop conditions they hit.
5. Spend Fable tokens on the decision layer: compare results, resolve conflicts,
   choose the implementation path, and review the final patch.

Prefer parallel subagents when the slices do not depend on each other. Keep
blocking or highly coupled work local.

## Handoff Packets

Write delegated prompts as if the subagent has no useful chat context. Include
only the context it needs:

- The repo path and exact objective.
- The files, packages, or surfaces in scope and anything explicitly out of
  scope.
- The evidence format to return: files, line refs, commands, diffs, failures,
  screenshots, and uncertainty.
- The verification commands or browser flows to run, plus what success should
  look like when that is knowable.
- Stop conditions: if the code does not match the prompt, a command fails after
  a reasonable retry, or the task needs out-of-scope files, stop and report
  instead of improvising.

## Vetting Delegated Work

Treat subagent reports as leads, not facts. Before using a high-impact finding,
opening a PR, or telling the user the work is done, Fable should reopen the
important cited files, confirm the relevant line refs or failures, and review
the final diff against the task. Let lighter agents gather signal; keep
truth-judgment with Fable.

## Common Scenarios

Treat these as soft defaults, not rigid rules:

- Research: ask lighter agents to scan docs, prior art, APIs, and repo surfaces;
  Fable decides what evidence changes the plan.
- Coding: give cheaper agents bounded edits or candidate patches; Fable owns
  shared-file coordination, integration, and final review.
- Testing: have Fable suggest the validation direction and the scripts or
  browser checks that matter. Let lighter agents run targeted tests, browser
  flows, screenshots, and log reduction, then report exact commands, failures,
  likely causes, and whether failures look flaky, environmental, or real.
- Debugging: use cheaper agents to cluster logs, reproduce issues, and try
  small fixes; Fable decides which diagnosis is most trustworthy.

If a task is tiny or the validation itself needs delicate judgment, keep it
with Fable.

## Diagram

Use `assets/fable-orchestrator.excalidraw` when a visual explanation helps.

## Claims

For codebase-heavy work, it is reasonable to describe this as up to 3-5x more
cost-efficient and 2-4x faster when independent research, coding, or testing
slices can run in parallel. Treat those as workload-dependent estimates, not
guarantees.

Good launch copy:

> Make Claude Fable more efficient by using cheaper subagents for token-heavy
> research, coding, and testing, saving Fable for judgment, architecture,
> synthesis, and final review.

input.prompt: How do you install or update the Entire CLI? Give the exact install commands shown (curl installer, brew, etc.). Also summarize in one sentence what Entire does.

content.content:
# Entire CLI Installation

**Install Command:**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | sh
```

The webpage doesn't mention a Homebrew installation option or update procedures—only the curl installer is provided.

**What Entire Does:**
Entire CLI integrates with your git workflow to automatically capture and index AI agent sessions alongside commits, creating a searchable record of "how code was written" with full context preserved in your repository's history.

content.content:
Enable Entire with session tracking for your AI agent workflows.

If Entire is not yet configured, this runs the full configuration flow.
If Entire is already configured but disabled, this re-enables it.

If the current directory is not a git repository, Entire can initialize one
for you and (optionally) create a matching GitHub repository via the gh CLI.

Usage:
  entire enable [flags]

Flags:
      --absolute-git-hook-path          Embed full binary path in git hooks (for GUI git clients that don't source shell profiles)
      --agent string                    Agent to set up hooks for (e.g., claude-code, codex, copilot-cli, cursor, factoryai-droid, gemini, opencode, pi; external agents on $PATH are also available). Enables non-interactive mode.
      --checkpoint-remote string        Checkpoint remote in provider:owner/repo format (e.g., github:org/checkpoints-repo)
  -f, --force                           Force reinstall hooks (removes existing Entire hooks first)
  -h, --help                            help for enable
      --init-repo                       If not a git repo, initialize one non-interactively
      --initial-commit-message string   Commit message for the initial commit when bootstrapping a new repo
      --local                           Write settings to .entire/settings.local.json instead of .entire/settings.json
      --no-github                       Initialize local git repo only; skip creating a GitHub remote
      --no-init-repo                    If not a git repo, exit instead of prompting to initialize one
      --project                         Write settings to .entire/settings.json even if it already exists
      --repo-name string                GitHub repository name for the new repo (used when bootstrapping)
      --repo-owner string               GitHub user or organization login for the new repo
===
Update non-agent Entire settings in the current repository.

Manages telemetry, git-hook installation mode, strategy options, and summary
provider configuration. Agent installation is handled by 'entire agent'.

Examples:
  entire configure                                # Show this help
  entire configure --telemetry=false              # Opt out of telemetry
  entire configure --absolute-git-hook-path       # Reinstall git hook with absolute path
  entire configure --force                        # Reinstall git hook
  entire configure --checkpoint-remote github:org/checkpoints
  entire configure --summarize-provider claude-code
  entire configure --summarize-timeout-seconds 300   # 5m deadline for explain --generate

content.text:
Entire is updated and set up. Summary:

**Update:** You had CLI 0.5.2; latest is **0.7.8** (released June 30). The official `curl | sh` installer was failing silently on an anonymous GitHub API rate limit, so I downloaded the v0.7.8 release directly with your authenticated `gh`, verified the sha256 checksum, and installed to `~/.local/bin`. I also installed `git-remote-entire`, a remote helper that ships in newer releases but wasn't in your old install.

**Setup (in the caravan repo):** Ran `entire enable --agent claude-code`, which installed 7 Claude Code hooks into `.claude/settings.json`, wrote `.entire/settings.json`, and created the `entire/checkpoints/v1` orphan ref where session metadata lives. `entire doctor` reports healthy. From now on, coding sessions in that repo get captured as checkpoints alongside commits — `entire explain` and `entire rewind` work against that history. I committed and pushed the config so it travels with the repo (the hooks are no-ops on machines without the binary, so this is safe for the public repo).

Two things you may want:

1. **Other repos**: setup is per-repo — run `entire enable --agent claude-code` in any other repo you want tracked (e.g. `~/fable-experiments` itself, or wrinkles).
2. **Cloud features**: checkpoints are stored locally in git refs; if you want the entire.io web experience you'll need to authenticate interactively — type `! entire login` in this session and it'll open the browser flow.

One note: these hooks take effect for *new* Claude Code sessions in that repo, so this session's work isn't captured; the next session in the caravan directory will be.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/marlin/.claude/skills/efficient-fable

# Efficient Fable

Use Claude Fable as the orchestrator, architect, synthesizer, and final judge.
Use cheaper subagents for token-heavy research, coding, testing, and
summarization that do not require Fable's full judgment.

## Where Fable Shines

Reserve Fable for:

- Decomposing ambiguous work into clean parallel slices.
- Architecture, product, and safety tradeoffs.
- Reading conflicting subagent reports and deciding what matters.
- Integrating partial implementations into one coherent plan.
- Final review, risk assessment, and user-facing synthesis.

## Delegation Pattern

1. Name the expensive-token risk: large repo search, long logs, broad docs, or
   repetitive edits.
2. Split independent work into subagents before reading everything yourself.
3. Use cheaper models for research scans, inventory, search summaries, narrow
   bug hunts, browser/testing passes, test output reduction, and bounded code
   edits.
4. Ask subagents for concise evidence: files, line references, commands run,
   diffs, uncertainties, and stop conditions they hit.
5. Spend Fable tokens on the decision layer: compare results, resolve conflicts,
   choose the implementation path, and review the final patch.

Prefer parallel subagents when the slices do not depend on each other. Keep
blocking or highly coupled work local.

## Handoff Packets

Write delegated prompts as if the subagent has no useful chat context. Include
only the context it needs:

- The repo path and exact objective.
- The files, packages, or surfaces in scope and anything explicitly out of
  scope.
- The evidence format to return: files, line refs, commands, diffs, failures,
  screenshots, and uncertainty.
- The verification commands or browser flows to run, plus what success should
  look like when that is knowable.
- Stop conditions: if the code does not match the prompt, a command fails after
  a reasonable retry, or the task needs out-of-scope files, stop and report
  instead of improvising.

## Vetting Delegated Work

Treat subagent reports as leads, not facts. Before using a high-impact finding,
opening a PR, or telling the user the work is done, Fable should reopen the
important cited files, confirm the relevant line refs or failures, and review
the final diff against the task. Let lighter agents gather signal; keep
truth-judgment with Fable.

## Common Scenarios

Treat these as soft defaults, not rigid rules:

- Research: ask lighter agents to scan docs, prior art, APIs, and repo surfaces;
  Fable decides what evidence changes the plan.
- Coding: give cheaper agents bounded edits or candidate patches; Fable owns
  shared-file coordination, integration, and final review.
- Testing: have Fable suggest the validation direction and the scripts or
  browser checks that matter. Let lighter agents run targeted tests, browser
  flows, screenshots, and log reduction, then report exact commands, failures,
  likely causes, and whether failures look flaky, environmental, or real.
- Debugging: use cheaper agents to cluster logs, reproduce issues, and try
  small fixes; Fable decides which diagnosis is most trustworthy.

If a task is tiny or the validation itself needs delicate judgment, keep it
with Fable.

## Diagram

Use `assets/fable-orchestrator.excalidraw` when a visual explanation helps.

## Claims

For codebase-heavy work, it is reasonable to describe this as up to 3-5x more
cost-efficient and 2-4x faster when independent research, coding, or testing
slices can run in parallel. Treat those as workload-dependent estimates, not
guarantees.

Good launch copy:

> Make Claude Fable more efficient by using cheaper subagents for token-heavy
> research, coding, and testing, saving Fable for judgment, architecture,
> synthesis, and final review.
